steps.title: One real solution → discriminant = 0
steps.content: Use the discriminant when the question is really asking how many real solutions a quadratic has. The sign of $b^2-4ac$ tells whether there are two, one, or no real solutions. For $81x^2 + \frac{18}{33}x + p = 0$, set $b^2 - 4ac = 0$: $$\left(\frac{18}{33}\right)^2 - 4(81)(p) = 0$$
